info.name: Vas-Hosting API
info.description:
Welcome to the VasHosting.cz API documentation! This API can be used for manipulating with servers, domains and all services related to domains (dns, emails, crons, etc.).

Feel free to give us some feedback and things you think our API could do.

## Response formats

Our api communicates only via JSON, we receive **JSON** body via the **POST** requests, and we provide back usually empty **204** response or response code **200** with **JSON** body if we need to provide you some information.

Successfull responses: **200** (with json), **204** (empty body).  
Error responses: **4xx** (with JSON, please read the "message" json key).

You can see examples of correct requests (with successful responses) and bad requests (with error responses) under each endpoint's demo.

## Variables in URLs

An example variable in url can be (your domain name) or (your server hostname), variables will tel us the context and they will setup the environment.

## Units

As we provide hosting for your domains and offer managed VDSes/VPSes, too, we need to handle with various units for storage and operational memories. As hosting tariffs for domains are usually used for smaller projects, it works with smaller units (like kB and MB) as stored data isn't too big. On the other side, managed servers often works with bigger data in GB and TB. To deal with this asymmetry we decided to use two unit systems in this API.

### Domains (and related services)

Everything around domains (includes all related services like databases, FTP or emails) uses **Megabytes (MB)** in endpoints of this API.

When we need to count with bigger numbers, we always divide by **1,000**, so e.g. **1 GB = 1,000 MB**.

### Servers (VPS, VDS)

When dealing with storage units and limitations around servers (VPSes and VDSes), always use **Gigabytes (GB)** for disk spaces and **Gibibytes (GiB)** for RAM size.

When we need to save higher unit, for instance TB, we will divide bytes by 1,000 for storage (server drives, etc.) and by 1,024 for RAM.

## Authorization

Every your request needs to be authorized, authorization is done via **API keys**, they can be created here [https://portal.vas-hosting.cz/api-key](https://portal.vas-hosting.cz/api-key) and scope is currently unlimited, they will be displayed only once, since we are encrypting them and **we cannot show them again**, so please save them to some secure place.

Authorizatin is done with http headers, header name is **X-API-Key** and the value is your api key.

item.name: Domains
Databases

response.name: Working example

request.description:
| **JSON Parameter** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| name (string) | yes | Database name |
| type (mysql/postgresql) | yes | Database software |
| password (string 8-64) | yes | User password |
| encoding (string) | no | MySQL/PostgreSQL encoding |
| note (string) | no | Note |

| **JSON Parameter** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| password (string 8-64) | yes | New password |

item.description:
## Units

All API endpoints for databases uses **Megabytes (MB)** as storage size units.

request.description:
| **JSON Parameter** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| name (string) | yes | Record name |
| content (string) | yes | Record content/value |
| type (string) | yes | Type (A/MX/TXT etc.) |
| ttl (int 60-86400) | yes | Time to live |
| priority (int 0-65535) | no | Priority for MX records |
| note (string) | no | Your internal note |

response.name: Account not found

item.description:
When working with email accounts, always use only account name that is before **@yourdomain.com**, because domain name is always specified in the URL, if you are manipulating with email [test@yourdomain.com](https://mailto:test@yourdomain.com), url for changing password will look like this:

```
https://centrum.vas-hosting.cz/api/v1/domains/yourdomain.com/email-accounts/test/change-password

 ```

## Units

All API endpoints for emails uses **Megabytes (MB)** as storage size units.

response.name: Incorrect version

item.description:
## Units

As we noted at the top of this API documentation, all units (maily around storage and its limitations) for domains and related services (everything in this section) are in **Megabytes (MB)**. As we count with jumping over content of this document, this information is repeated in all subsections.

item.name: Servers

request.description: Returns all created labels and their colors. These labels could be assigned to your servers for better identification, grouping or filtering. [Filtering servers](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#6b537ccb-f16f-416a-92b3-17ea3c869e0e) by their labels is supported directly through our API.

response.name: Working example

request.description:
Creates new server label.

Name of the label (key `name`) can't contain any whitespace characters. It's restricted due to some limitations of working with labels in GUI.

response.name: Invalid color
Name collision
Name with whitespace chars

request.description: Deletes an existing server label. If the label has been assigned to some servers, it is **automatically unassigned** before deletion. So, be careful which label you delete.

response.name: Non-existent label

request.description:
Modifies server label.

Name of the label (key `name`) can't contain any whitespace characters. It's restricted due to some limitations of working with labels in GUI.

response.name: Name collision
Invalid color
Non-existent label for editing
Name with whitespaces

item.name: Get list of supported operating systems

request.description: Returns list of OSes we can automatically install for you. These operating systems could be used in API requests for server installations). If you need some special OS, you need to contact us for manual intervention.

response.name: Working example

item.name: Get list of assigned IP addresses

request.description: Returns your assigned IP addresses that can be used for server installs.

response.name: Working example

query.description: Optional query parameters for filtering by label name

request.description:
Returns all active servers in customer's account.

## Filtering

This endpoint supports an optional **filtering**.

### By label name

You can filter servers by labels that were assigned to them before. This could be done using optional query parameter `labels[]`, which takes an array of label names. You can support one or many labels this way, but it always has to be specified like an array query parameter.

**Correct usage:**

- `labels[]=my-label` – Selects servers with label "my-label".
- `labels[]=first&labels[]=second` – Selects servers with both "first" and "second" labels.
    

**Wrong usage:**

- ~`label=my-label`~ – The correct name of the query parameter is `labels[]`, even for single label.
- ~`labels=my-label`~ – You need to provide query parameter as array always, even for single label.
- ~`labels[]=first,second`~ – When specifying two labels, you need to insert `labels[]` query parameter twice. This example would select filters with label named "first,second".

response.name: Working example
Working example with filtering by labels
Working example
Insufficient permissions

request.description: Returns all VDS available for authenticated customer.

response.name: Working example

request.description:
Allows to install VPS (Virtual Private Server) on VDS (Virtual Dedicated Server). Dedicated servers could be partitioned to more virtual servers, when there is enough capacity of CPU, RAM, storage and the partitioning is allowed (you need to contact us, if you need to use this feature).

**Used units (and value restrictions):**

- `cpu` — GHz (greater than 3)
- `ram` — GiB (greater than or equal to 2)
- `primaryStorageSize` — GB
- `primaryStorageLocation` — number of VDS's disk slot (see [https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#b5d554ff-1355-4ef0-bd71-2c8bb07ae465](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#b5d554ff-1355-4ef0-bd71-2c8bb07ae465))
- `operatingSystem` — OS identifier (see [https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#493137a1-9d6f-44b9-8209-911897af75d2](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#493137a1-9d6f-44b9-8209-911897af75d2))
- `ipAddress` — Ip address (optional, see [https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#da892cd7-e364-439d-9080-765f0cd1082c](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26076792/2s93CRJque#da892cd7-e364-439d-9080-765f0cd1082c))

item.description:
## Units

Every numbers for defining storage sizes are in **Gigabytes (GB)**, RAM sizes are in **Gibibytes (GiB)**.
